description: When everything says no, nowhere can say yes.
goal: (-> (forall x (not (App P x))) (not (exists x (App P x))))

hints.text: The reverse quantifier-negation law: if everything fails to be $P$, then nothing can be $P$. $\forall x. \lnot P(x) \to \lnot \exists x. P(x)$. The proof goes: assume something exists, open the [witness|witness], use the universal to get a [contradiction|contradiction], and close. Start by assuming $\forall x. \lnot P(x)$.
To prove $\lnot \exists x. P(x)$ — i.e. $\exists x. P(x) \to \bot$ — assume the existential.
Open the existential with a fresh [witness|witness] $y$ using `fix`.
Instantiate the universal (step 1) with the [witness|witness] term $y$ using `forall-elim` to get $\lnot P(y)$.
$\lnot P(y)$ is $P(y) \to \bot$. Apply [modus-ponens|modus ponens] on step 5 ($\lnot P(y)$) and step 4 ($P(y)$) to reach $\bot$.

Close the existential with `exists-elim`: the existential (step 2), the [witness|witness] subproof (step 6), and the witness variable $y$. The engine [assumption|discharges] both the witness [atom|atom] and $P(y)$.
[assumption|Discharge] the existential to get $\exists x. P(x) \to \bot$ — that is, $\lnot \exists x. P(x)$.
[assumption|Discharge] the universal to finish. When everywhere is wrong, nowhere is right.
